name: Microsoft 365 Core MCP Server
what_it_does: Community MCP server for managing Microsoft 365 services including distribution lists, security groups, Teams channels, SharePoint sites, and Exchange mailbox settings. Enables AI agents to automate M365 administration tasks — creating groups, managing memberships, configuring Teams, and handling user/license operations.
use_cases: Automating M365 group and distribution list management from IT admin agents
Managing Teams channel creation and membership from collaboration agents
Querying and configuring SharePoint sites from content management agents
Handling Exchange mailbox settings and policies from email admin agents
not_for: Reading personal email content (use personal email MCP servers)
Consumer Microsoft accounts (designed for M365 Business/Enterprise)
best_when: An IT admin or DevOps agent needs to automate Microsoft 365 tenant administration — creating groups, managing Teams, configuring Exchange — via Microsoft Graph API.
avoid_when: You need to read individual email content or calendar events for end-users (scope is M365 admin operations, not personal productivity). Use Microsoft's official Graph API MCP for broader coverage.

auth.notes: Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) OAuth2 app registration required. App-level permissions via Microsoft Graph API. Admin consent required for most M365 management scopes.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — Microsoft Graph API access included with M365 subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: None for API; M365 subscription required
pricing.notes: Microsoft Graph API is included with M365 subscriptions. MCP server is free open source. M365 subscription required.

performance.rate_limits: Microsoft Graph throttling applies; 10,000 requests per 10 minutes per app

known_agent_gotchas: PRIVACY: M365 tenant data is highly sensitive — corporate communications, user data, org structure
Admin consent for Microsoft Graph scopes is complex — plan Azure AD app registration carefully
Graph API throttling can be aggressive for batch operations — implement exponential backoff
Community server (DynamicEndpoints) — not Microsoft official; verify compatibility with your M365 version
agent_readiness.security_notes: PRIVACY: Corporate M365 tenant data. OAuth2 with admin consent. Use minimal Graph API permissions. Community implementation — audit code before production enterprise use.

security_score_components.security_notes: Corporate tenant data. OAuth2 admin consent. Principle of least privilege critical. Community implementation — verify before enterprise deployment.
